<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 3 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- These files are build-tool inputs, not runtime modules — they form the static asset root consumed by the Vite build rather than the import graph
- Zero cohesion and zero coupling metrics are expected for a zone composed entirely of binary assets and an HTML template, which have no JS import relationships
- As the viewer's HTML entry point, index.html is architecturally significant despite appearing disconnected from the import graph
- Zero cohesion and coupling are correct and expected — binary assets (PNG logos) and an HTML entry point have no JS import edges, so community detection cannot measure structural connectivity for this zone.
- The zone is correctly isolated from the JS import graph; any future additions here (favicon, web manifest, splash screens) should remain static assets only to preserve this clean boundary.
- Themed logo variants (darkmode/lightmode) are present but the switching mechanism lives in the viewer JS layer — confirm that the CSS or JS dark-mode toggle references both assets so neither becomes dead weight at build time.
- The zone is unreachable by the boundary-check policy tests in web-boundary-tests because it has no JS import edges — policy tests that assert on import graphs cannot catch misplacement of binary assets; asset placement is only enforced by build configuration
- The logo files are consumed by the Vite build but their dark/light switching mechanism lives in the viewer JS layer — if the CSS or JS toggle ever references only one logo variant, the other becomes a dead build artifact with no static analysis tool to catch it
- Binary asset zone (PNG logos + HTML entry) is invisible to import-graph policy tests — the boundary-check suite cannot detect if a non-asset file is accidentally placed here, making asset zone integrity enforcement build-config-only

</insights>
